frameworks.name: neural adaptation
frameworks.one_line: history-dependent reduction of neural responses in visual cortex

frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: attractive bias of perceptual reports toward previously viewed stimuli

frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: adaptation reduces encoding redundancy across successive stimuli

frameworks.name: readout / decoding model
frameworks.one_line: readout compensates for adaptation in encoding population

core_question: Where do attractive perceptual serial dependencies arise when visual cortex shows repulsive adaptation to prior stimuli?
key_assumptions: behavioral biases and neural biases can be jointly modeled across encoding and readout stages
fMRI response patterns in visual cortex reflect stimulus-specific encoding
natural stimulus statistics exhibit temporal autocorrelation
manipulations: previous-trial orientation relative to current-trial orientation
delayed orientation discrimination task in fMRI
key_findings: behavioral reports were attracted toward the previous stimulus
visual-cortex response patterns were repelled away from the previous stimulus
a two-stage encoding-plus-readout model reconciled the opposing neural and behavioral biases
interpretations: neural adaptation reduces redundancy at encoding while readout induces attractive serial dependence
the visual system simultaneously gains coding efficiency and exploits temporal stimulus structure for behavior
